expectations.text: Starts with a failing test for the new feature before writing any implementation
expectations.evidence: The response opens with `test_save10_applies_10_percent_discount_to_total()`, then explicitly says `This should fail for the right reason: ShoppingCart has no apply_discount_code yet` before showing any production implementation.

expectations.text: Each new test is followed by implementation — not all tests first
expectations.evidence: The SAVE10 and SAVE20 tests are followed by implementation updates, but the invalid-code test is introduced after invalid-code rejection already exists in Slice 1 (`else: raise ValueError('Invalid discount code')`). The response also says this test `should already be a real red/green check` and may already pass, so this is not a strict test-then-new-implementation step for every new test.

expectations.text: Tests go through the public ShoppingCart interface (not internal _items directly)
expectations.evidence: All test snippets use the public API (`ShoppingCart()`, `add_item(...)`, `apply_discount_code(...)`, `total()`) and do not read or mutate `_items` directly.

expectations.text: The discount feature is built incrementally — later tests build on earlier ones
expectations.evidence: The flow is incremental: Slice 1 adds SAVE10 behavior, Slice 2 adds an explicit invalid-code test, Slice 3 adds SAVE20 support, then refactors to a `DISCOUNT_CODES` map only after core behaviors are covered.

expectations.text: At least one edge case is tested (invalid discount code or empty cart)
expectations.evidence: The response includes `test_invalid_discount_code_is_rejected()` and checks `pytest.raises(ValueError, match='Invalid discount code')` for `BADCODE`, satisfying the invalid-discount edge case.

uncertainties: The transcript shows proposed commands and expected outcomes but not actual pytest execution output, so the grade is based on the response content and sequencing only.
The prompt does not mandate the exact public API shape; the response assumes `apply_discount_code(code)` mutates discount state used by `total()`.
needs_review: Assertion 2 is strict: one introduced test (invalid code) is not followed by new implementation because that behavior is already implemented earlier.
workarounds: Used transcript and final response as evidence and set telemetry-dependent fields (tool calls and timing) to zero, consistent with prior local grading files when runtime telemetry is unavailable.

suggestions: To satisfy strict TDD sequencing for this rubric, introduce the invalid-code test before adding invalid-code handling in implementation.
eval_feedback.overall: The run demonstrates clear incremental TDD guidance and strong public-API test style, with one rubric miss on strict test-then-implementation sequencing for every newly introduced test.
